root_cause.text: The archive extraction routines (zip and tar) did not validate whether the destination path of the extracted entries remained within the intended target directory, making them vulnerable to directory traversal attacks (Zip Slip) via malicious archive entries containing relative paths like `../`.
root_cause.evidence: The patch adds checks in `FileUtil.java` (e.g., `if (!file.getCanonicalPath().startsWith(targetDirPath))`) to throw an `IOException` if the extracted file would be created outside the target directory, and adds a test case (`testUnZip2`) specifically verifying that a zip entry named `../foo` is rejected.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The archive extraction routines (zip and tar) did not validate whether the destination path of the extracted entries remained within the intended target directory, making them vulnerable to directory traversal attacks (Zip Slip) via malicious archive entries containing relative paths like `../`.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch adds checks in `FileUtil.java` (e.g., `if (!file.getCanonicalPath().startsWith(targetDirPath))`) to throw an `IOException` if the extracted file would be created outside the target directory, and adds a test case (`testUnZip2`) specifically verifying that a zip entry named `../foo` is rejected.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add boundary check to prevent Zip Slip vulnerability when expanding zip archives.
intent_record.what: Added canonical path validation for zip entries against the target directory path.
intent_record.how: Throws an IOException if the zip entry resolves to a path outside the destination directory, preventing path traversal attacks.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add path traversal validation to prevent Zip Slip vulnerabilities during extraction.
intent_record.what: Added a canonical path check against the target directory before extracting files from a ZIP archive.
intent_record.how: Throws an IOException if a ZIP entry's extracted path would fall outside the intended target directory, preventing path traversal attacks (Zip Slip).

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a path validation guard to prevent directory traversal vulnerabilities during tar extraction.
intent_record.what: Added a canonical path check to verify that the extracted tar entry resides within the target output directory.
intent_record.how: Prevents directory traversal attacks by throwing an IOException if an archive entry attempts to extract files outside the intended destination.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove local outputFile instantiation to utilize a previously validated file path.
intent_record.what: Removed the local instantiation of the outputFile variable from this block.
intent_record.how: Relies on the outputFile being instantiated and validated earlier in the method, preventing potential path manipulation issues.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add static import for JUnit fail method.
intent_record.how: Makes the fail method accessible directly within the file without class qualification.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add import for StandardCharsets to support character encoding.
intent_record.what: Added an import statement for java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets.
intent_record.how: Makes the StandardCharsets class available for use in the current file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix typos and remove misleading comments in the testUnZip method.
intent_record.what: Modified code comments by removing a typo and an incorrect reference to a tar file, replacing them with a single corrected comment.
intent_record.how: This change has no impact on program behavior at runtime since it only affects source code comments.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix comment to accurately refer to unzip instead of untar.
intent_record.what: The word 'untar' was replaced with 'unzip' in an inline comment.
intent_record.how: This change has no effect on runtime behavior; it solely improves the accuracy of the code documentation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add test case to verify rejection of zip entries that attempt directory traversal.
intent_record.what: Added a new test method testUnZip2 to test zip file extraction behavior with malicious paths.
intent_record.how: Ensures that attempting to extract a zip entry with a path containing '../' correctly throws an IOException, preventing directory traversal.

grouping_result.intent: Add a path validation guard to prevent directory traversal vulnerabilities during tar extraction.
Remove local outputFile instantiation to utilize a previously validated file path.
Add static import for JUnit fail method.
Add import for StandardCharsets to support character encoding.
Fix comment to accurately refer to unzip instead of untar.
Add test case to verify rejection of zip entries that attempt directory traversal.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add path validation guards to prevent directory traversal and Zip Slip vulnerabilities during archive extraction.
Fix typos and update comments to accurately refer to unzip instead of untar in the test methods.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly implements path validation checks during archive extraction (both zip and tar) to prevent directory traversal (Zip Slip), which exactly matches the root cause.

The group modifies the extraction process to use a previously validated file path instead of instantiating a potentially unsafe local file path, directly addressing the directory traversal (Zip Slip) vulnerability.
The group only adds a static import for a test method, which is completely unrelated to the directory traversal vulnerability.
Adding an import for character encoding does not address the directory traversal (Zip Slip) vulnerability in archive extraction.
The group only contains comment updates and typo fixes in test methods, which do not address the directory traversal vulnerability.
The group adds a test case specifically designed to verify the fix for the directory traversal (Zip Slip) vulnerability, making it directly related to the vulnerability's root cause.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: YARN-2185. Use pipes when localizing archives. Contributed by Miklos Szegedi
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 7288b08330001102f944124d18e02bf0585d3e96
Author: Jason Lowe <jlowe@apache.org>
Date:   Wed Jan 31 09:39:43 2018 -0600

    YARN-2185. Use pipes when localizing archives. Contributed by Miklos Szegedi

diff --git a/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/apache/hadoop/fs/FileUtil.java b/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/apache/hadoop/fs/FileUtil.java
index 4d971aa8156..bf9b1463782 100644
--- a/hadoop-common-project/hadoop-common/src/main/java/org/apache/ha
bic_trace_step.reason: The newly added `unZip(InputStream inputStream, File toDir)` method iterates over zip entries and uses `File file = new File(toDir, entry.getName());` without validating if the resulting `file` is still within `toDir` (e.g., checking `file.getCanonicalPath().startsWith(toDir.getCanonicalPath())`). This allows malicious entries like `../` to write outside the intended directory, which is the root cause of the vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
HADOOP-4687 Moving src directories on branch


git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/hadoop/core/branches/HADOOP-
bic_trace_step.reason: The 'unZip' function creates a new File object using 'entry.getName()' (line 472: `File file = new File(unzipDir, entry.getName());`) without validating if the resulting path remains within the intended 'unzipDir'. Similarly, the 'unTar' function blindly extracts archives using a shell command without validating entry paths. This makes both methods vulnerable to directory traversal attacks.
